Patch Set 1: Verified+1

Passed enforced test review-dne-part-3 on RHEL 8.8/x86_64 uploaded by Trevis Autotest 2 from trevis-34vm1: https://testing.whamcloud.com/test_sessions/e225fddc-ad95-4622-87a3-554910b9ae14 ran 3 tests.

Patch Set 1: Code-Review+1

Feels a bit strange the kernel's max depth being 5 while e2fsck's being 8, but just works for us this time.

Patch Set 1:

> Feels a bit strange the kernel's max depth being 5 while e2fsck's
 > being 8, but just works for us this time.

There's and old programming saying - "be strict in what you send, and relaxed in what you accept", in order to improve interoperability. It is possible that e2fsck is running a patched kernel, like ours, and possibly a kernel modified years in the future.
Change has been successfully cherry-picked as b156790dea16b3c494f5130666a9293fa179fe99 by Oleg Drokin
